ud1.3:1.2:
on one occasion, the Blessed One was staying at Uruvelā on the
bank of the Nerañjarā River at the root of the Bodhi tree–
the tree of awakening–newly awakened.
ud1.3:1.3:
And on that occasion he sat at the root of the Bodhi tree for seven days in one session,
sensitive to the bliss of release.
ud1.3:1.4:
Then, with the passing of seven days, after emerging from that concentration, in the third watch of the night,
he gave close attention to dependent co-arising in forward and reverse order, thus:
ud1.3:2.1:
When this is, that is.
From the arising of this comes the arising of that.
ud1.3:2.2:
When this isn’t, that isn’t.
From the cessation of this comes the cessation of that.
In other words:
ud1.3:2.3: From ignorance as a requisite condition come fabrications.
ud1.3:2.4: From fabrications as a requisite condition comes consciousness.
ud1.3:2.5: From consciousness as a requisite condition comes name-&-form.
ud1.3:2.6: From name-&-form as a requisite condition come the six sense media.
ud1.3:2.7: From the six sense media as a requisite condition comes contact.
ud1.3:2.8: From contact as a requisite condition comes feeling.
ud1.3:2.9: From feeling as a requisite condition comes craving.
ud1.3:2.10: From craving as a requisite condition comes clinging/sustenance.
ud1.3:2.11: From clinging/sustenance as a requisite condition comes becoming.
ud1.3:2.12: From becoming as a requisite condition comes birth.
ud1.3:2.13:
From birth as a requisite condition, then aging-&-death, sorrow,
lamentation, pain, distress, and despair come into play.
ud1.3:2.14: Such is the origination of this entire mass of suffering & stress.
ud1.3:3.1: Now from the remainderless fading and cessation of that very ignorance comes the cessation of fabrications.
ud1.3:3.2: From the cessation of fabrications comes the cessation of consciousness.
ud1.3:3.3: From the cessation of consciousness comes the cessation of name-&-form.
ud1.3:3.4: From the cessation of name-&-form comes the cessation of the six sense media.
ud1.3:3.5: From the cessation of the six sense media comes the cessation of contact.
ud1.3:3.6: From the cessation of contact comes the cessation of feeling.
ud1.3:3.7: From the cessation of feeling comes the cessation of craving.
ud1.3:3.8: From the cessation of craving comes the cessation of clinging/ sustenance.
ud1.3:3.9: From the cessation of clinging/sustenance comes the cessation of becoming.
ud1.3:3.10: From the cessation of becoming comes the cessation of birth.
ud1.3:3.11: From the cessation of birth, then aging-&-death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, distress, and despair all cease.
ud1.3:3.12: Such is the cessation of this entire mass of suffering & stress.
ud1.3:4.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud1.3:5.4: as the sun, illumining the sky.
